element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic tank operation and movement mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Tactical map and platoon command interface
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to coordinate four M1 tanks as a platoon unit
Critical survival strategy emphasized throughout campaign

element_introduction_order.element: Support unit coordination (recon helicopters, Bradley IFVs, MLRS artillery)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced tactical layer requiring management beyond four core tanks
Punishes static positioning; teaches dynamic repositioning
Shift from defensive holding to aggressive waypoint capture

element_introduction_order.element: Crew skill progression and survival incentives
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Long-term resource management; encourages tactical caution

typical_relief_triggers: Successful terrain positioning reducing incoming fire
Support unit arrival alleviating immediate threat
Enemy attrition reducing overall battlefield pressure
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy wave attacks (numerically superior Warsaw Pact forces)
Transition to offensive objectives requiring exposure
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asymmetric wave-based pacing with defensive early campaign escalating to offensive pressure; tension driven by resource scarcity (crew survival) and positional vulnerability rather than time pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Support units availability varies by mission; player can choose to engage or conserve them

difficulty_curve.early_game: Defensive posture against numerically superior enemies; teaches positioning and terrain awareness using technological superiority of M1 tanks
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mixed defensive/offensive with introduction of support unit coordination and consequence management (artillery punishment for static positioning)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Primarily offensive with waypoint objectives requiring aggressive NATO counterattack across Rhine; accumulated crew skill provides advantage
notable_spikes: Transition from defensive to offensive objectives increases exposure risk
Artillery barrage mechanic creates sudden catastrophic damage potential
Loss of skilled crew members permanently reduces combat effectiveness

notable_level_patterns.description: Early campaign missions emphasize defensive positions against enemy waves; success through terrain positioning and damage mitigation rather than offensive action
Missions grant access to varied support units (helicopters, Bradley IFVs, MLRS, M60 tanks) with tactical map ordering; effectiveness scales with proper unit synergy
Late campaign shifts to offensive operations requiring NATO forces to advance and hold/reach waypoint objectives; increased exposure versus defensive phase
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Rhine crossing offensive scenarios (unnamed specific level)

notable_level_patterns.description: Surviving crew members between battles gain rank and skill; creates meta-incentive for tactical caution and unit preservation across campaign arc
